channel.title: ForkJoin on Kevin wang's blog
channel.description: Recent content in ForkJoin on Kevin wang's blog

item.title: Java Fork/Join Framework
item.description: The Fork/Join Framework (java.util.concurrent) supports structured parallelism: a problem is split recursively into subtasks that run in parallel, partial results are merged, and the caller waits for completion. Tasks are lightweight ForkJoinTask objects executed by a fixed pool of worker threads using work-stealing deques — not one thread per task. This document traces the OpenJDK implementation in ForkJoinPool, ForkJoinTask, and related classes.
